channel.title: HSJ - Latest news
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for HSJ latest news

item.title: Neighbourhood plans ‘in danger’, says top five trust leader
item.description: The government’s neighbourhood health agenda is “in danger of not happening” amid a lack of clarity over governance structures and funding, the chair of England’s fourth-largest trust has claimed.

item.title: CQC warns Health Bill could leave it fighting itself in court
item.description: The Care Quality Commission has warned that government plans for it to absorb the national patient safety investigations body could leave it arguing against itself in the High Court.

item.title: Dozens of stroke units lose ‘A’ ratings
item.description: Close to 80 per cent of stroke units are falling well short against a swathe of new standards introduced to the high-profile national audit, according to HSJ analysis.

item.title: CEO returns to English trust after four years in Wales
item.description: The CEO of a Welsh health board is taking over a large mental health and community trust.

item.title: ‘Central pressure’ forcing ICBs to cut reform investment
item.description: Integrated care boards are struggling to protect funds to deliver neighbourhood health and other 10-Year Health Plan proposals this year – with one cluster forced to withdraw a £33m fund days after launching it.

item.title: HSJ Digital Awards 2026: Winners revealed
item.description: The HSJ Digital Awards 2026 took place last night at the ICC Birmingham, celebrating outstanding contributions to digital healthcare transformation across the NHS and wider sector.

item.title: Six ICBs chosen to lead specialised commissioning
item.description: Just one of the seven NHS England regions has yet to decide which organisation will host the patch’s joint commissioning of specialised and some other services.

item.title: Former chair ‘sensationalised whistleblowing claim to oust CEO’
item.description: An employment tribunal has thrown out a former chair’s whistleblowing claims against a trust CEO, saying he “misrepresented and exaggerated” concerns as part of a campaign to oust her.

item.title: ‘New hospitals’ paired with builders for projects worth £14bn
item.description: Eleven “new hospitals” have been matched with construction firms to build their projects, valued at up to around £14bn, HSJ has learned.

item.title: Exclusive: Trust pleads guilty after patient fire death
item.description: A trust has pleaded guilty to fire safety offences relating to a patient’s death in a rare case where a fire service has brought a prosecution against an NHS provider, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: GP tactics in contract dispute ‘really positive’, claims NHSE director
item.description: The British Medical Association’s advice that GPs audit the data sharing agreements they are involved in as part of the union’s dispute with government is actually “really positive” and a “helpful” thing to do, NHS England’s primary care director has said.

item.title: Trust sued by staff over ‘hazardous’ exposure to gas
item.description: Dozens of staff members working in a major hospital trust’s maternity unit are seeking compensation over claims they were exposed to “hazardous” levels of gas and air.

item.title: ‘Operational efficiency’ AI tools part of new £900m procurement push
item.description: AI systems designed to reduce costs and improve outcomes through “workflow automation” have been included in an NHS procurement framework worth up to £900m.

item.title: New CEO for trust recovering from abuse scandal
item.description: A mental health trust that has been battling to recover from an abuse scandal has appointed a new chief executive.

item.title: Revealed: ‘Catastrophic’ gaps in tech regulation plans
item.description: Proposed amendments to UK medical device regulations are “a disgrace” and risk creating the lowest barrier to entry for high-risk AI devices in the developed world, sector experts have told HSJ.

item.title: Mapped: RTT waiting times
item.description: An interactive map of local NHS waits around England in March 2026, showing the pressures, with links to all the details by organisation and specialty.

item.title: Controversial group model could be scrapped
item.description: Two trusts that formed a controversial group model are launching a review, which could lead to the partnership being split up.

item.title: Single patient record could save £110m, says DHSC
item.description: The new single patient record will save £110m over the next 10 years, according to an impact assessment accompanying publication of the NHS bill.

item.title: Exclusive: Southport attack victim accuses trust of ‘cover up’ over care records breach
item.description: Hospital staff inappropriately accessed the medical records of victims of the 2024 mass stabbing at a dance class in Southport, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: Ministers take powers to overrule ‘unresponsive ICBs’
item.description: The government is taking a raft of new powers over the NHS – including one that will enable them to direct integrated care boards on virtually any of their functions – in its new Health Bill, published on Thursday.
